data.name: Subtitle
data.value: Tools for testing and verifying system components.

data.name: IngestionBody
data.value: Trigger one ingestion cycle right now. Normally this runs automatically every 30 minutes.

data.name: IngestionFootnote
data.value: OpenAQ fetches up to 50 stations within 25 km of Bishkek center, then drops readings older than 7 days before saving.

data.name: ContainerImportBody
data.value: Imports waste containers from OpenStreetMap via the Overpass API for Bishkek bounding box.

data.name: ContainerImportFootnote
data.value: Existing imported containers are updated in place by their OSM id; new ones are added with a generated OSM-XXXXX code. Manually-added containers are not touched.

data.name: BackfillReportsBody
data.value: Resolves the district for every report with a null district_id using the point-in-polygon algorithm and the seeded district boundaries. Reports outside every polygon stay null.

data.name: BackfillContainersBody
data.value: Resolves the district for every waste container with a null district_id so future "container is full" IoT tasks auto-route to the right district inspector. Containers outside every polygon stay null and fall back to the broadcast notification path.

data.name: ReseedBoundariesBody
data.value: Rewrites the polygon points of all four Bishkek districts in place using the current spec in BishkekDistrictsSeeder. Preserves district IDs and inspector assignments — only the boundary points are replaced.

data.name: ReseedBoundariesFootnote
data.value: Run this after editing the boundary spec (e.g. to switch from simplified rectangles to irregular polygons). The in-memory cache is invalidated automatically; existing reports keep their district_id values, so consider running "Backfill districts" afterwards if the new shapes substantially reassign coordinates.

data.name: ReseedFromGeoJsonHeading
data.value: Reseed district boundaries from GeoJSON (real OSM)

data.name: ReseedFromGeoJsonBody
data.value: Replaces all four district polygons with the real OSM-derived boundaries shipped as Districts/Snapshots/bishkek-districts.geojson. Same idempotent transactional write path as the hand-tuned reseed above — district IDs and inspector assignments are preserved.

data.name: ReseedFromGeoJsonFootnote
data.value: The file is validated strictly before any DB write: exactly 4 Polygon features with codes LENIN, PERVOMAY, SVERDLOV, OKTYABR; if validation fails the existing boundaries stay intact. After running, consider Backfill districts so historic reports near a moved edge re-resolve.
